findings.issue: Broken actions/checkout ref syntax in .github/workflows/remote-hive-build.yml. Erroneous search-and-replace corrupts the '@v4' action tag/ref, pointing to an invalid local file path.
findings.remediation: Change all occurrences of '- uses: actions/checkout @crates/services/src/services/config/versions/v4.rs' to '- uses: actions/checkout@v4'

findings.issue: Broken/truncated shell script syntax and regex matching in Verify PNPM_VERSION plumbing step. The `jq` command lacks closing parenthetical and quotes, contains a space in `sub("^pnpm @...` and wrong file argument logic.
findings.remediation:
Restore the correct shell command:
EXPECTED_VERSION=$(jq -re '.packageManager | sub("^pnpm@"; "")' package.json)

findings.issue: Broken/truncated step and syntax errors in Build remote-server image step of docker-build job. It lacks closing parenthetical and quotes, and entirely omits the `docker compose build` commands.
findings.citation: .github/workflows/remote-hive-build.yml:74 — export PNPM_VERSION=$(jq -re '.packageManager | sub("^pnpm @package.json ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE still present in build log" followed by unaligned braces/exists.
findings.remediation:
Restore the step's complete script:
run: |
  set -o pipefail
  export PNPM_VERSION=$(jq -re '.packageManager | sub("^pnpm@"; "")' package.json)
  cp crates/remote/.env.remote.dev crates/remote/.env.remote
  echo 'LOOPS_EMAIL_API_KEY=ci-placeholder' >> crates/remote/.env.remote
  (cd crates/remote && docker compose --env-file .env.remote build --no-cache remote-server 2>&1 | tee /tmp/ci-build.log)
  ! grep -q "ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE" /tmp/ci-build.log || {
    echo "FAIL: ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE still present in build log"
    exit 1
  }
  echo "BUILD PASSED: no ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE"

findings.issue: Invalid pnpm package name in root Dockerfile due to erroneous space. The space causes `npm install` to fail as it treats `@${PNPM_VERSION}` as a separate, invalid package name.
findings.citation: Dockerfile:31 — RUN npm install -g pnpm @${PNPM_VERSION} && pnpm install
findings.remediation:
Remove the space between `pnpm` and `@${PNPM_VERSION}`:
RUN npm install -g pnpm@${PNPM_VERSION} && pnpm install

findings.issue: Invalid format for corepack prepare command in remote Dockerfile due to erroneous space. Corepack fails when trying to parse the version specification containing a space.
findings.remediation:
Remove the space between `pnpm` and `@${PNPM_VERSION}`:
&& corepack prepare "pnpm@${PNPM_VERSION}" --activate

findings.issue: Broken PNPM_VERSION extraction in rebuild.sh due to space in sub pattern. Since the prefix 'pnpm@' in package.json does not have a space, the sub regex '^pnpm @' fails to match, leaving 'PNPM_VERSION' set to 'pnpm@10.25.0' instead of '10.25.0'. This propagates to corepack as 'pnpm@pnpm@10.25.0', crashing the build.
findings.remediation:
Remove the space in the sub match regex:
export PNPM_VERSION=$(jq -re '.packageManager | sub("^pnpm@"; "")' ../../package.json)
